derivation_note: Inherited verbatim from plate_calibrations/storm-routines (Cycle 18 agent calibration, verified). Ground-plane values below are estimated by visual inspection of the plate (no camera-calibration tooling exists in this repo) -- treat as art-directed estimates, not measured facts. Location-level calibration: panels shot on this plate inherit it.

calibration.reference_object: near streetlamp base plinth (wide cylindrical bollard-like base of the right-foreground cyan streetlamp)
calibration.note: Base plinth foot-flare bottom sampled at y=580, top of the plinth collar cap at y=460 (verified against a 3x overlay zoom -- marker spans the plinth exactly). Chosen as the scale proxy because the ~1 m plinth reads at chibi character scale at that depth; the full lamp pole is far too tall to anchor a chibi.

ground_plane.horizon_derivation: MEASURED via same-height repeated objects: three streetlamps of matching design at different depths. Near cyan lamp base y=580 h=500px, mid cyan lamp base y=477 h=172px, left magenta lamp base y=498 h=223px. Pairwise H=(hn*yb_far-hf*yb_near)/(hn-hf) gives ~424 (near/mid), ~433 (near/left), ~406 (mid/left). Cross-checks: right curb line from the far road corner (712,452) through (840,505)-(1240,700) converges at street center x~630 around y~403-418; left/right building wall-ground joins converge ~435-444. Painted plate, so lines do not converge perfectly; adopted 425 (+/-20 band).

light_sources.note: Large cyan tube fixture on the right-foreground streetlamp; dominant light, casts a visible cyan cone down-left onto the roadway.
Pink tube fixture on the mid-left streetlamp; magenta fill and puddle bounce on the left half of the street.
Double pink neon strips on the right building face (part of the zig-zag neon dressing); broad magenta bounce onto the right sidewalk and road.

weather.note: Storm-wet night street: no falling-rain streaks drawn in the plate itself, but the whole roadway is rain-slick with large sheet puddles reflecting the cyan/magenta neon.

reflective_surfaces.note: Entire visible roadway is a wet sheet with cyan/pink neon reflections. Right edge follows the curb from the far road corner (712,452) through (840,505)/(1240,700), exiting at the bottom-right corner. Left roadside is not perfectly straight in the painting (apron near the left lamp), so the left edge is a traced polyline; the lower-left ambiguity is covered by the overlapping sidewalk pool polygon.
Large pink/teal flood pool over the lower-left sidewalk corner.
Bright teal reflection pool straddling the curb/gutter at the lower-right corner; strongest specular pool in the plate, overlaps the road sheet polygon by design.

camera.shot_scale: wide establishing, one-point perspective straight down the street

decompression_note: Was MZ-2026-09-02_P02_PANEL01 in the 24-panel edition; same locked beat, renumbered by the 16-page/96-panel format directive.
